messages.content: Need team members for this hackathon
Anyone who's into AI/ ML development or data engineering?
im lowk beginner tier but im down if you haven’t found anyone
which year are you graduating in? only 2026-27 batch can apply
can I get a rundown on how this goes?

You can get the info I have in the challenge page. But basically, it's a two stage hackathon: 1. 3 day virtual hackathon from 11th to 13th 2. People who clear that round will go on to present their solution to the judges in a virtual finale on 17th
The PS and dataset will be provided by them, along with the evaluation metrics
Top 50 teams will get PPI and internship opportunities for applied scientist role
I can share the team invite if you want
because only 4-5 hours are left for registration 😭

So  creating a Pin option for chatgpt or to say any other chat interface for llm,

Problem :
When you text too much with llm , then the chat gets too long, and if you want to go to a particular question again to see smtg , you have to scroll up again and keep whirling your eyes to see that heading , if you have faced this issue , you will know,

Solution: A chrome extension that allows you to pin texts , just like in whatsapp ,they have pin texts ,and by selecting them , you can scroll to that point.

Hmmmm
Well, do you know how shell scripts basically are just “do this, then do that then after that, do that other thing”?

Imagine that but for managing multiple computers at once
Installing, uninstalling packages, running commands, etc
You can set multiple ip addresses to manage and run/create roles to apply them
The project enforces a strict separation of data and logic, so devs are supposed to write 1 singular .py file with logic for it to work anywhere
Other than that, it supports builtin documentation provided by the same plugins, it supports secret management with sops and different key providers

Also it has a builtin encrypted runbook to document shit
Team support with git (handles secrets and these run books as well) and is composable through piping
It has data collection for observability and I’m working on a TUI for a centralised server for system management
Oh, and you can use the secrets/runbook management as a “pass” of sorts
OH, and you can write plugins that allow you to get dynamically sourced fleets of machines, not just hardcoded ones

Hey everyone 👋
I’m planning to build a healthcare-related hackathon project and I want it to feel like a real working startup — something practical, useful, and not just a demo.

Hey — I help early-stage teams build scalable full-stack systems without overcomplicating their cloud setup.

Mostly Java/Spring Boot on the backend, React/TS on the frontend. I care a lot about clean architecture, performance, and keeping infra costs sane as you grow.

Built 7+ production projects in the last couple years. Also built a custom 3D space engine that randomly blew up on Reddit (~500k views), which was fun.

If you’re building something and want someone who thinks long-term about architecture, happy to chat.

Hey @Board of Free Will Inc.  👋,
I’m building a full-scale multi-vendor e-commerce platform (customer app + vendor portal + admin dashboard) and I’m looking for people interested in collaborating on the frontend side.
Tech stack:
🌐 React / Next.js (Web – Vendor & Admin portals)
📱 React Native (Customer mobile app)
I know the name “e-commerce project” sounds common 😅 — but this one goes much deeper and includes some exciting and challenging features like:
Multi-vendor architecture
Real-time updates
AI-powered review summarization
Coupons & rule engine
Event-driven backend (Kafka)
Redis caching
Omnichannel notifications (Push, Email, WhatsApp, SMS)
Automation workflows
This is meant to be a serious, production-grade project, not just a basic CRUD store. Interested people can DM me or reply here

embeds.description:
hey guys i need some idea, i'm planning to try and make a dependency manager for c. something like npm for c. rn i haven't started thinking about it in engineerng terms or anything. just trying to get some ideas and see which ones i can implement. the how i'll see to myself.

currently i plan to go either of the two routes:
1. download dependency from github repo or gnu savannah repo locally into the project folder
2. download dependency from github repo or gnu savannah repo globally into a dedicated folder and symlink it into the current project. also provide functionality to download locally if asked for.

the dependency manager would try to also handle automatically generating makefiles for the project and updating them as new dependencies are added.

it would (naturally) force a directory structure so that it can actualyl build YOUR project files, but i do think it'd be worthwhile to allow the user to configure the same (i.e. the project structure).

i now ask you guys to give me some ideas of what ya'll wanna see and what ideas of mine might be wrong or something. suggestions and feedback is all that is asked for.
